_note: Batch 4: 20 additional real, web-researched Israel programs gathered 2026-07-07, deliberately targeting categories under-represented in batches 1-3 (pluralistic/egalitarian learning, youth-movement gap years beyond the US, professional/advocacy fellowships, niche academic tracks, arts, sports) since pre-army mechinot (24), Modern-Orthodox/Hesder men's yeshivot (~26), women's seminaries (~20), and the main teen-summer/Masa-flagship rosters are already saturated across the existing 153 programs (105 in DB + 48 in batch 3). Every candidate was run through a description-and-org-level fuzzy dedup check against all 153, not just a slug check (see CLAUDE.md's 'Adding real programs' section for why that matters). Stopped at 20, not 50: roughly two dozen other leads were researched and rejected as not reputable/verifiable/active/on-target for this wiki's audience, including ROI Community (Schusterman is sunsetting it in July 2026), Israel Tech Challenge/ITC (all programs discontinued per the org's own notice), the Wexner Israel Fellowship (ended ties with Harvard, no 2024+ cohort), Momentum/JWRP (wrong audience -- mothers of young children, not young adults), Sinai Retreats (US-based retreat center, no confirmed Israel component), AICF and Kohelet Policy Forum (grantmaking/think-tank orgs, not a discrete immersive program), Yad Vashem internships (informal, no structured admissions track), AJEEC-NISPED and MEET (local Israeli/Palestinian teen programs, no diaspora-facing admissions track), and several college international offices (Herzog, Tel-Hai, Achva, Zefat, Beit Zvi) too thin on English-language / overseas-student specifics to write an honest entry. Where a cost or contact detail could not be verified from a current public source, the field is left as an empty string rather than guessed -- see inline notes in the enrichment files for what's flagged. durationType is one of TEN_DAY|SUMMER|SEMESTER|GAP_YEAR|CUSTOM.

pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.name: Pardes Institute of Jewish Studies - Year/Semester Program
pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.description: A pluralistic, egalitarian beit midrash for university graduates combining classic Jewish text study (Talmud, Tanach, Halacha) with philosophical, ethical and societal questions facing the Jewish people today, taught seminar-style with chavruta (paired) learning; students choose from a wide range of electives and can study for Elul (September), a semester, or the full year while living independently in Jerusalem.
pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.durationText: Elul (3-4 weeks), 1 semester, or full year
pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.signupInstructions: Apply online via pardes.org.il; rolling admissions. A Masa Israel grant plus Pardes-specific financial aid (need- and merit-based grants and interest-free loans) can reduce cost for eligible students.

pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.name: Conservative Yeshiva
pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.description: An egalitarian yeshiva offering one-year, pre-college, advanced-studies, summer and winter Torah-study tracks for adults of all backgrounds and denominations, blending traditional beit midrash learning with a Conservative/Masorti approach; tuition includes Thursday day trips and a Shabbaton, and long-term study is Masa-eligible.
pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.signupInstructions: Apply through fuchsbergcenter.org. All full-time applicants are automatically considered for the Schindler Scholarship Fund; Masa grants and need-based aid available for eligible students.

pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.name: Hevruta Gap-Year Program
pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.description: An immersive Jerusalem gap year for 70 recent high school graduates -- half from North America, half from Israel -- blending Jewish text study, philosophy and modern thought with leadership training and communal life, drawing on the Hartman Institute's pluralistic scholarship and methodology.
pluralistic_egalitarian_learning.cost: $33,000 (includes housing, meals, all programming, trips, staff support and health insurance); limited need-based financial aid available

youth_movement_gap_years.name: Arevim - Masa Year of Service
youth_movement_gap_years.description: An 8-10 month program for young adults (18-21) combining Sherut Leumi-style social-service volunteering (health, education or welfare, participant's choice) with an educational program on Israeli-Arab conflict, politics and current affairs, leadership/entrepreneurship seminars, and cultural/outdoor activities, while living independently in rented Tel Aviv apartments.
youth_movement_gap_years.signupInstructions: Apply through the Masa Israel program listing or hholamit.org.il.

youth_movement_gap_years.name: FZY Year Course
youth_movement_gap_years.location: Jerusalem and Bat Yam (Tel Aviv area)
youth_movement_gap_years.description: Britain's flagship Zionist-youth-movement gap year: a 9-month program (or 4-month option) split between a Jerusalem semester and a Tel Aviv-area semester based in Bat Yam, combining academic study, community volunteering, direct engagement with Israeli society and a Special Interest Month (options include kibbutz, yeshiva, hiking, business internship, or surfing).
youth_movement_gap_years.signupInstructions: Apply through fzy.org.uk/yearcourse or the Masa Israel program listing.

youth_movement_gap_years.name: Habonim Dror UK - Shnat
youth_movement_gap_years.location: Kibbutz (Shorashim phase) and an Israeli city (Anafim phase)
youth_movement_gap_years.description: An 8-month gap year for UK school leavers built around Socialist-Zionist and collective-living values: 3 months on kibbutz (Shorashim) for group bonding, Hebrew ulpan and Jewish/Zionist education; a month of leadership training; and 4 months (Anafim) living communally in an Israeli city, volunteering, running youth groups, teaching English, and a March of the Living trip to Poland.
youth_movement_gap_years.signupInstructions: Contact Habonim Dror UK via habodror.org.uk to apply.

youth_movement_gap_years.name: Noam - Drachim Gap Year
youth_movement_gap_years.organization: Noam (Masorti Judaism's Zionist youth movement) / Masorti Olami
youth_movement_gap_years.location: Kibbutz Hannaton Education Center, Galilee (and other sites)
youth_movement_gap_years.description: A Masorti/Conservative-movement gap year for young adults from Noam's global network (UK, Europe, Latin America and beyond), combining Zionism, Masorti Judaism, tikkun olam, hadracha (leadership) and democracy education, run through Kibbutz Hannaton's Education Center in the Galilee.

youth_movement_gap_years.name: Maccabi Gap Year
youth_movement_gap_years.location: Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, and kibbutz placements
youth_movement_gap_years.description: A 5-10 month post-high-school program combining Machon Le'Madrichim leadership seminars, an optional taste of IDF Marva training, Magen David Adom EMS training, kibbutz living and work, touring across Israel, and a Poland trip focused on Shoah education; fully supervised by Maccabi World Union madrichim.
youth_movement_gap_years.signupInstructions: Apply through azabbg.bbyo.org/experiences/maccabi-gap-year or the Masa Israel listing for Masa Maccabi Gap Year.

youth_movement_gap_years.name: Kol Ami Mechina
youth_movement_gap_years.organization: Kol Ami - The Jewish Peoplehood Leadership Academy
youth_movement_gap_years.description: A 6-month leadership program (with an optional 3+ month 'Perek Bet' internship extension) bringing young Israelis together with Jewish peers from around the world to study Jewish Peoplehood, public diplomacy and Jewish texts alongside personal-growth work and volunteering that benefits Israeli society.
youth_movement_gap_years.durationText: 6 months (end of August-end of February), with an optional 3+ month extension
youth_movement_gap_years.signupInstructions: Apply through kolami.org.il or the Masa Israel mechina program listings.

professional_and_advocacy_fellowships.name: Israel Government Fellows (IGF)
professional_and_advocacy_fellowships.organization: Menachem Begin Heritage Center, in cooperation with the Office of the Prime Minister of Israel and Masa Israel Journey
professional_and_advocacy_fellowships.description: A 10-month elite government and policy internship placing Fellows in Israeli government ministries and NGOs for hands-on public-administration work, paired with intensive Hebrew study, tours, and weekly seminar days meeting Israeli officials, policymakers, journalists and activists. Founded in 2007 to strengthen the Diaspora-Israel connection and global Jewish leadership.

professional_and_advocacy_fellowships.name: Hasbara Fellowships Israel Training Program
professional_and_advocacy_fellowships.description: A 16-day leadership and advocacy training program for North American college-student leaders, active on 95+ campuses, combining ten workshops on Israel advocacy and campus leadership with meetings with Israeli political, military and business figures and conversations with Israelis and Palestinians across the political spectrum, in order to return as trained pro-Israel campus advocates.

niche_academic_programs.name: ELI Gap Year Program
niche_academic_programs.description: A new (launching 2026-27) pre-college gap year combining a minimum of four for-credit Tel Aviv University courses with cybersecurity, AI and entrepreneurship workshops, hands-on research in TAU labs, mentorship from Israeli entrepreneurs, Jewish-peoplehood programming, and immersive tours across Israel.
niche_academic_programs.durationText: Full academic year (August-May) or a single semester
niche_academic_programs.cost: $14,000/semester ($28,000/year); includes housing, insurance, tours and full academic credit, excludes flights/food/personal expenses
niche_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply via gapintl@tauex.tau.ac.il; fall 2026 deadline June 1, 2026, spring 2027 rolling.

niche_academic_programs.name: Technion American Medical School (TeAMS)
niche_academic_programs.organization: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology, Ruth and Bruce Rappaport Faculty of Medicine
niche_academic_programs.description: An English-language M.D. program (running since 1983) for U.S. and Canadian pre-med graduates who intend to practice in North America, combining basic medical sciences at the Technion with clinical education at leading Israeli medical centers; graduates sit U.S./Canadian licensing exams.
niche_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through teams.technion.ac.il; requires a bachelor's degree and MCAT score.

niche_academic_programs.name: Jerusalem College of Technology - International English Speakers Program
niche_academic_programs.description: A hybrid program letting English-speaking students combine a post-high-school yeshiva experience with a real academic degree: Torah/yeshiva studies in English in the morning, and English-taught coursework toward a Computer Science or Business Administration degree in the afternoon, with optional Hebrew ulpan.
niche_academic_programs.durationText: 1-year program, or full multi-year degree track
niche_academic_programs.signupInstructions: Email esp@jct.ac.il or apply via jct.ac.il/international.

niche_academic_programs.name: Ono Academic College International School
niche_academic_programs.description: An International School offering English-taught and hybrid English-Hebrew degree programs at a 23,000-student college, including an International Law LLB for international students, Olim and globally minded applicants seeking a legal education grounded in Israeli, comparative and international law, with support gradually integrating students into Israeli academic life.
niche_academic_programs.durationText: Full degree program (multi-year); semester options vary by track

niche_academic_programs.name: Shenkar College International Program
niche_academic_programs.organization: Shenkar College of Engineering, Design and Art
niche_academic_programs.description: A semester-long option for international students 18+ to study at a college combining engineering, design and art under one roof, taking English-taught courses plus hands-on art/design workshops conducted in simplified Hebrew; the International Office also runs exchange, internship and transdisciplinary-project partnerships with schools like Cornell and Yale.

niche_academic_programs.name: Emunah College - Faculty of Arts and Design
niche_academic_programs.description: An accredited arts and design degree program for religious women, covering visual art, visual communication and drama, with ESL/EPIC (English for Purposes of International Communication) coursework supporting the English proficiency required for an Israeli academic degree.

arts.name: Buchmann-Mehta School of Music International Program
arts.organization: Tel Aviv University, in partnership with the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra
arts.description: A Bachelor's/Master's/Artist Diploma program (established 2008) for outstanding foreign musicians in instrumental performance, voice, conducting or composition, taught by IPO principal players and other leading Israeli musicians, with performance opportunities in the school's Symphony Orchestra, chamber ensembles and bilingual (Hebrew/English) opera productions.
arts.durationText: Bachelor's (4 years), Master's (2 years), or Artist Diploma
arts.cost: Full-tuition scholarship for all accepted international students; a limited number also receive a living stipend and/or housing scholarship

sports.name: Israel Lacrosse Gap Year Program
sports.description: A gap year for lacrosse players ages 18-30 to live, train and compete in Israel for a college semester or full year, playing in domestic league and exhibition games plus tournaments abroad in Europe, completing a formal coach-certification program and mentoring local youth teams as ambassadors for the sport.

outreach_beginner_learning.name: EYAHT - Aish HaTorah College for Women
outreach_beginner_learning.description: A full-time college for advanced Jewish learning aimed at unaffiliated, college-educated and professional English-speaking women ages 22-30, founded in 1984, offering multi-level classes in Jewish philosophy, history, prayer, Chumash and Rabbi Noah Weinberg's '48 Ways to Wisdom' curriculum; most students are exploring becoming more observant.
outreach_beginner_learning.signupInstructions: Contact Aish HaTorah's Jerusalem women's programs office via aish.com/jerusalem_programs_for_women.
